reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal problem. The hypothesis `P.degree = n` makes explicit the implicit assumption that $c_n$ is the leading coefficient (natural reading of the notation $P(x) = c_n x^n + \cdots + c_0$). `Polynomial.aeval r P = 0` correctly encodes $P(r) = 0$. The conclusion iterates over $i \in \{0, \ldots, n-1\}$ (the $n$ indices), and the inner sum $\sum_{j=0}^{i} P.\text{coeff}(n-j) \cdot r^{i+1-j}$ expands exactly to $c_n r^{i+1} + c_{n-1} r^i + \cdots + c_{n-i} r$, matching each of the $n$ described sums. The `∃ m : ℤ, m = sum` clause (with the implicit ℤ → ℚ coercion) expresses "the rational sum equals an integer", matching "are integers". Nat subtractions $n-j$ and $i+1-j$ are safe since $j \le i \le n-1$.
